response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist software engineer (web, mobile, desktop, some embedded) with strong software craftsmanship and TDD background, but the role demands SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU/DNN workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design at the principal level. None of these core competencies appear in the resume. While there is tangential low-latency (DLang microservices) and some C/C++/embedded work, there is no evidence of chip architecture, performance modeling for AI accelerators, or deep-learning optimization. The educational path (BS in CS) could satisfy the BS+8 route, but the domain mismatch is decisive.
strengths: Deep, varied engineering experience including some C/C++ and embedded/low-latency work
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, and demonstrated teaching/mentoring ability
Comfortable across many languages and the full stack
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or hardware architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD background
Profile is web/application-focused, not the systems/silicon performance domain required

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No deep-learning workload or GPU architecture experience
